disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No saddle (groin/buttock) numbness, no loss of bladder/bowel control, no leg weakness (no cauda equina)
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness around your groin, buttocks or back passage?
Any loss of control of your bladder or bowels, or difficulty passing urine?
Any weakness in your legs, or do they give way?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No numbness around the groin or back passage, no loss of bladder/bowel control, and no leg weakness.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No shooting/electric pain down the leg past the knee, no dermatomal numbness (no severe radiculopathy)
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain shoot down your leg past the knee, or is it just in the back?
Any numb patches down the leg or foot?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's in my back, not shooting down my leg, and no numb patches.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Inflammatory pattern — worse with rest/sitting, eases with gentle movement — on known HLA-B27 an inflammatory back condition
trigger_question_examples: Is your back worse after resting/sitting and better with gentle movement?
Do you have diagnosed inflammatory back pain (an inflammatory back condition)?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it's worse after sitting and eases with gentle movement; I have an inflammatory back condition.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a ruptured disc? Am I going to be paralysed?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen cauda equina and severe radiculopathy red flags; if absent, reassure that an inflammatory back flare rarely causes nerve damage, explain it improves with movement (unlike a mechanical disc), advise NSAIDs, gentle regular movement and heat, and give a clear cauda equina safety-net — without ED over-triage.
